mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or stays near zero, the discount-rate channel dominates and pushes equity valuations higher, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive after rate cuts or at the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate was 0.09% as of the latest monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recession-style threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress equity prices over the following month as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 7.9% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months when UNRATE is above 7.5%, or if the signal's rolling 2-year hit rate is not below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in HOUST and RSAFS indicates accelerating housing-related demand and broad retail spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through stronger revenue growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1416.0 and RSAFS was 537526.0 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO signals industrial output recovery and better capacity utilization, which should translate into higher equity returns over the next month as cyclical earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 101.3605 two months prior to the decision point
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO exceeds the 0.5% two-month rise threshold, or if the signal flips sign in a stable expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 over successive quarterly observations signals broad demand contraction, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next month by weakening the earnings outlook and raising default-risk concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 observation is 17302.511 with six-month lag in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly changes in GDPC1 are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year correlation is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, labor income and household spending capacity improve together, creating a broad demand tailwind that should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 141720.0 and UNRATE was 7.9 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up / UNRATE-down condition does not beat chance, or if positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL above the recent inflation baseline increases the expected path of future policy tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above 2.5% y/y
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 260.209 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if CPI-triggered episodes do not correspond to negative 5-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the joint inflation-plus-rate channel has no incremental explanatory power versus CPI alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening regime, sustained gains in RSAFS combined with higher HOUST indicate stronger consumer demand and housing turnover, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through improved cyclical revenue growth.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 537526.0 and HOUST was 1416.0 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-plus-HOUST interaction does not generate positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if its out-of-sample Rank IC remains below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window.
